title: dryopteris affinis subsp. pseudodisjuncta
intro: Dryopteris affinis subsp. pseudodisjuncta is a subspecies of the Dryopteris affinis fern, a type of vascular plant native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Dryopteris affinis subsp. pseudodisjuncta include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and promote overall respiratory health. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or extracting the active compounds with a solvent like ethanol.
